elections.note: Certified final 226,259 = Election-Day 108,416 + Vote-by-Mail 117,843 (CA SoS 2010 Statewide General, Statement of Vote -> Voter Participation Statistics by County; turnout 65.30% of 346,516 registered). Election-night FIRST report (~8:05 PM) not recoverable: San Mateo's live results lived at shapethefuture.org/elections/results/nov2010/ and the Wayback Machine's earliest capture is results.asp at 2010-11-04 22:37 UTC (Nov 4, two days after the Nov 2 election) -- a post-election cumulative, not the election-night first tranche; no Nov 2-3 snapshot exists and news quotes no first-report ballot count. Pre-epollbook (SMC deployed e-pollbooks with the Nov 2018 Voter's Choice Act rollout); ASV never adopted.

Certified final 288,592 = Election-Day 119,212 + Vote-by-Mail 169,380 (CA SoS 2012 SOV, Voter Participation Statistics by County; turnout 79.83% of 361,486 registered). First report not recoverable: results were on racetracker.shapethefuture.org (summary_results.aspx); the Wayback Machine's earliest capture is 2012-11-09 18:17 UTC (Nov 9, three days after the Nov 6 election) -- a late cumulative, not the election-night first tranche. Pre-epollbook; ASV never adopted.

Certified final 164,453 = Election-Day 54,056 + Vote-by-Mail 110,397 (CA SoS 2014 SOV, Voter Participation Statistics by County; turnout 46.25% of 355,598 registered). First report not recoverable: racetracker.shapethefuture.org (summary_results.aspx); the Wayback Machine's earliest capture is 2014-11-07 15:21 UTC (Nov 7, report instance iID=11, three days after the Nov 4 election) -- a late cumulative. Pre-epollbook; ASV never adopted.

Certified final 323,303 = Election-Day 105,014 + Vote-by-Mail 218,289 (CA SoS 2016 SOV, Voter Participation Statistics by County; turnout 81.66% of 395,928 registered). First report not recoverable: racetracker.shapethefuture.org; the Wayback Machine's earliest capture is 2016-11-12 (report instances iID=14-15, four days after the Nov 8 election) -- a late cumulative. Pre-epollbook; ASV never adopted.

Certified final 290,058 = Election-Day/Vote-Center 34,005 + Vote-by-Mail 256,053 (CA SoS 2018 SOV, Voter Participation Statistics by County; turnout 72.63% of 399,351 registered). First report not recoverable: this was SMC's first Voter's Choice Act general (vote centers + e-pollbooks). Per smcacre.org's published schedule the 8:05 PM first report posted a portion of VBM received through Nov 5, then 9:00 PM and half-hourly updates. Results lived on apps.smcacre.org/raceTRACKER, whose earliest Wayback capture is 2018-11-24 03:49 UTC (report instance iID=44, 18 days post-election); the smcacre.org results landing page was only captured the morning after (2018-11-07 12:11 UTC). No election-night first-report capture exists. POST-epollbook (Nov 2018 = adoption year); ASV never adopted.

CAVEAT -- not the 8:10 PM first tranche. Certified final 252,233 = Election-Day/Vote-Center 17,961 + Vote-by-Mail 234,272 (CA SoS 2022 SOV, Voter Participation Statistics by County; turnout 58.29% of 432,734 registered). Election-night value 122,135 is the registrar's FINAL ELECTION NIGHT REPORT from the livevoterturnout.com ENR (rendered 'Website Updated 11/9/2022 3:17:37 AM -- SEMI-OFFICIAL RESULTS, FINAL ELECTION NIGHT REPORT', Ballots Cast 122,135 = Vote by Mail 106,626 + Vote Center 15,509), recovered with render_wayback.cjs from the earliest Wayback capture of the ENR Index page (2022-11-09 17:42 UTC); Wayback archived no 8-9 PM snapshot. This is the END-OF-NIGHT total, NOT the documented 8:10 PM first report (VBM-only; vote centers begin reporting at 9:00 PM), so 122,135/252,233 = 48.42% is an UPPER BOUND -- the genuine first-report share is materially lower and should not be compared directly to counties whose value is a true 8 PM first tranche. POST-epollbook; ASV never adopted.

CAVEAT -- 10:00 PM report, not the 8:05 PM first tranche (but close). Certified final 337,241 = Election-Day/Vote-Center 39,466 + Vote-by-Mail 297,775 (CA SoS 2024 SOV, Voter Participation Statistics by County; turnout 76.07% of 443,350 registered). Election-night value 182,925 is the registrar's 10:00 PM report from the livevoterturnout.com ENR (rendered 'Website Updated 11/5/2024 9:59:57 PM -- SEMI-OFFICIAL RESULTS, 10:00 P.M. REPORT', Ballots Counted 182,925 = Vote by Mail 178,796 + Vote Center 4,129), recovered with render_wayback.cjs from the earliest Wayback capture of the ENR Index page (2024-11-06 06:42 UTC = 10:42 PM PT); Wayback archived no 8-9 PM snapshot. Vote-center ballots are only 4,129 (~2% of the total), so this 10 PM figure is only slightly above the 8:05 PM first VBM tranche (~179k) -- 182,925/337,241 = 54.24% modestly overstates the genuine first-report share. POST-epollbook; ASV never adopted.

notes: Non-COVID PRE-2020 baseline (2010, 2012, 2014, 2016, 2018) plus 2022 and 2024; the 2020 general is deliberately EXCLUDED as a COVID/early-voting outlier. Definition: election-night % = ballots in the registrar's FIRST post-poll-close (~8 PM PT) report divided by CA SoS certified final ballots cast. DENOMINATORS are solid PRIMARY for all seven years -- CA Secretary of State Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County ('Total Voters' = Election-Day/Vote-Center + Vote-by-Mail): 2010=226,259; 2012=288,592; 2014=164,453; 2016=323,303; 2018=290,058; 2022=252,233; 2024=337,241. NUMERATORS are the weak point for this county: San Mateo's first-report (8:05 PM) ballot count is NOT recoverable for ANY year because the Wayback Machine never captured the live election-night results pages during the 8 PM-to-midnight window. For 2010-2016 results lived on shapethefuture.org / racetracker.shapethefuture.org and the earliest archived captures are all 3-4 days post-election (late cumulatives, not first reports); for 2018 (apps.smcacre.org/raceTRACKER) the earliest capture is 18 days post-election; news coverage quotes no first-report ballot totals -- so 2010-2018 election_night_ballots are null/confidence none. For 2022 and 2024 the livevoterturnout.com ENR Index pages WERE archived but only LATER on election night, so the recovered numbers are the FINAL ELECTION NIGHT REPORT (2022: 122,135 at 3:17 AM, includes all vote-center ballots) and the 10:00 PM REPORT (2024: 182,925), NOT the 8 PM first tranche; both are tagged confidence secondary and are UPPER BOUNDS on the true first-report share (2022's gap is large -- ~15k vote-center ballots; 2024's is small -- only ~4k vote-center). Adoption: San Mateo deployed electronic pollbooks alongside its first Voter's Choice Act all-mailed-ballot / vote-center election in November 2018, so 2010-2016 are PRE-epollbook and 2018/2022/2024 are POST; automated signature verification (ASV) was NEVER adopted, so vs_asv = 'n/a' for every election. Because the recoverable 2022/2024 numbers are later-night reports rather than 8 PM first tranches, do NOT directly compare San Mateo's election_night_pct to counties (e.g. SF, Orange) whose values are genuine first reports.
